reviews.review_description: Unfortunately I wasn’t able to play the first claw machine.  It would not let me load the any coins.  I tried restarting my headset with no luck.  Graphics is great.

Very Nice game, the Bear is too cute ! And i love

platform games!

Edit : After rejumping into the game. I feel the game deserves an extra half star (3.5 for me but meta doesnt do half stars)

Extra levels plus tweaks to the claw machine


A cute family friendly adventure for kids of all ages.

The stages are generally split up into segments where you play an arcade claw like mini game collecting enough teddy bears to play a stage.

Followed by the platforming section where you guide teddy through a diorama like section which involved light platform and some.
 scene manipulation (think Moss)

There are also a handful of "other puzzles" are dotted between the earlier levels.
These typically are you sliding squares/manipulation of segment to bounce the light style puzzles.

At the times of writing there are 9 levels for you to complete with a modest run time.
However the game has launched in "Early Access" with promises of further levels to be added.

With a few more levels added to the game, Bearly Escape could be a wonderful introduction to younger players into VR.
The diorama like levels are unlikely to cause motion sickness in new players and the animals are all so cute and cuddly!

developer_response.body: Hi, there! We really appreciate your thoughtful words. Knowing that you enjoyed guiding the teddy bears through both puzzles and platforming is a big encouragement for our team. Reviews like yours motivate us to keep refining the experience so that each stage feels even more fun and memorable!

reviews.review_description: Fun wholesome light puzzle platform adventure

This game is not really in a genera that i play much, but i was still able to enjoy some parts of the game. The biggest problem for me was the unskippable cutscenes which i frankly found very boring as i did not feel invested in the story at all, however it did have my favourite thing in games: GAMBLING

developer_response.body:
Hi, thank you for sharing your feedback — we really appreciate you giving the game a try even though it’s not your usual genre!

We’re sorry to hear the unskippable cutscenes affected your experience. The good news is we’re planning to add a “skip cutscene” option in a future update to give players more flexibility, especially for those who prefer to dive straight into the gameplay.

We’re glad you enjoyed the “gambling” mechanic with the claw machine — we’re continuing to refine it to make it even more fun and rewarding. You will have great experience with it final launch on July 24.

Thank you again for playing and helping us improve during Early Access.

reviews.review_title: Has potential to be a 5 but...
reviews.review_description: I started playing but couldn't get past level 4. Finished the level and it didn't do anything. So in order to redo it I have to do the claw machine all over again. Which cost more coins. I really like the game but the claw machine before every level is annoying. I like the puzzles way better. And in level 4 the height is off and won't readjust. When I climb the wall I can't see over it to finish second time I played it which is why I give it 2 stars. I understand early access but For $15 should be way better. Update: Also going through the reviews I see where devs respond to 4 stars and positive comments but not others. So I'm taking it down to 1 star at this point.

developer_response.body:
Hi there, thank you so much for sharing your honest feedback — we really appreciate it.

We’re very sorry the current experience didn’t meet your expectations. The game is still in Early Access, and we’re actively working on improvements and fixing issues like the level 4 height adjustment bug and the flow between claw machine and puzzle stages.

The full release is scheduled for July 24, which will bring significant upgrades and optimizations to address many of the pain points players have raised.

We sincerely apologize for the frustration this caused and hope you’ll consider giving it another try at the end of the month — we’d love to hear what you think of the improved version.

BTW，you could collect more coins in the achievement and collection parts.

reviews.review_description:
Well now, let me tell you a bit about this cute little game.

So you start off as a Park Ranger, (tell me that doesn’t seem like the coolest, most calming job ever…and I actually know one! Weird irony, I know…) whose sweet pup Is missing and you head out to go find the little guy. Turns out that there is this evil scientist who is taking animals and turning them into the little plushies, so you need to find these little cuties and save them.
What’s really cute is the sweet bear you come across in the woods who’s grabbing a little snooze who, when you pet him, wakes and runs away. But, you follow him, pull him out of a hole and befriend him…(or should I say “BEARfried” him? Ha! See what I did there? Ya know…Bear instead of…ok yeah…ummm never mind…)…so he leads you the scientist’s various labs where the plushies are in a big ol’ claw machine for you to rescue.

Ok. So. Let’s just say the only “claws” I want associated with me might be in the descriptions my exes use when…oh wait…TMI… sorry… ahem…The claw machine is never what I’m gonna be making a beeline to, anywhere, because the utter frustration of those things drives me insane, lol. Now this one…it’s fun, but sometimes I would grab a little guy only to have it slip out and then suddenly I was the Hulk! But, I do enjoy the challenge! And so will you! This claw is fun and you will love it!  Just remember to get as many bears as you can because those are extra lives and you’re gonna need them in between the labs when you get to the platforming part of the game.

That part of the game is where you’re now controlling the bear, and are going to be jumping around on the beautiful grassy little hills and cliffs, and trying to not only break honey pots and crates to gather coins, (which you will need for the claw machine and other things), but also you will find the little animals that are being held captive, and you’ll need to save them there as well.

All of this, put together with some little puzzles to get you into the labs themselves, really offer up a fun and enjoyable experience. And as I know this game may seem like it’s meant for younger folks, as an adult who admittedly loves watching cartoons, (yes, even as friends doth mocketh me greatly), I really found myself having a great time. This was a fun experience for me, and I appreciate the challenges involved that weren’t insurmountable, (as sometimes many games tend to make them), allowing me to move forward while feeling a sense of accomplishment.

So frankly, I’d say this is a fun game for all ages and if you are a family that loves to play VR games together, although this isn’t a multiplayer, I would say it’s definitely something to add to your game library, because it is suitable for all ages and I’m sure everyone in the clan would find enjoyment in playing it. Hey…your kids may even start asking for a pet bear instead of a puppy for Christmas! (Ha! Have fun with THAT one Mom and Dad!)…
Have fun and enjoy!

This is a fun platformer with some minor collecting elements, the levels are broken up into nice small segments. In between the platforming there are some short puzzles as well as a crane game to help break up the action. Would recommend to anyone looking for a platform experience, this is also a cute style family friendly game for anyone who is looking for games in that style.

developer_response.body: Hi Sandwichx, thank you so much for your thoughtful review and kind recommendation! We're glad to hear you enjoyed the structure of the levels and the balance between platforming, puzzles, and claw machine segments. Our goal has been to create a fun and accessible experience for players.

reviews.review_description:
Bearly Escape is impressive for a game that's still under development (early access). This single-player adventure puzzle game is presented from a blend of first-person and third-person perspectives. Your mission is to rescue adorable animals who’ve been turned into plush toys by an evil scientist.

You step into the shoes of a Forest Ranger, which is basically like being a superhero but with more flannel and fewer capes. Your trusty sidekick, an old hound who’s gone mysteriously AWOL (probably chasing squirrels), has left you with nothing but a reconnaissance drone equipped with a grabbing claw. Yes, a claw machine, but instead of winning cheap toys, you’re actually saving your furry friends.

To break into Dr. Kidd’s secret labs, you’ll need to solve puzzles that are so simple even a toddler could crack them (but let’s be honest, toddlers are basically geniuses). Each time you rescue a plushie from the claw machine in the first person perspective, you gain necessary tools and health to help you and your lil bear buddy on the platformer escapades. 

When in the third-person perspective, you'll guide your lil bear buddy through a platformer jungle while he encourages you to keep going. "Yeah, buddy, I’m doing my best here dodging all these traps and foes that look like they can turn you into ground up bear meat!"

As you journey deeper, the claw machine cranks up the difficulty— because what’s an adventure without a little stress and rage inducing frustration? ;)

The game dazzles with eye-popping visuals and colors that make your eyeballs do a happy dance. You can earn upgrades for your claw machine and bear buddy, unlocking abilities that will make you feel like the ultimate plushie whisperer. Sure, there are only 9 levels and about 2 hours of gameplay, but there's more to come.

Compete and replay levels to snag the fastest completion time on the leaderboard— because, ummm, what grown man doesn’t want to brag about their cute plushie collection?

developer_response.body: Hello MissChiefVR, thank you for the fantastic and entertaining review! We're thrilled that you connected with the humor, pacing, and charm of Bearly Escape — your description gave our team a big smile. We’re continuing to build on what’s already there, with more levels, features, and plush-saving chaos on the way. Your support and enthusiasm mean a lot to us, and we can’t wait to show you what’s next! 😀

reviews.review_description:
This game is a fun platformer mixed with puzzles and claw machines.

If you like claw machines, you're in luck! You play them between every level. If you like platformers, you're in luck! You play a level between each claw machine session. And if you like mini puzzles mixed in, they've got those too.

If you don't like the claw machines, the good news is that it's pretty quick. These increase in difficulty as you go, which helps keep it a little interesting. Also, the physics are surprisingly solid. There wasn't a time where I thought I should've gotten something and didn't (excluding the times it would've happened in a real claw machine). 

The levels get more complex as you go, which is a necessity for platformers. I didn't make it all the way through the game yet, but I can't wait to see what's in store!

developer_response.body: Hi Zman1011, thank you for your thoughtful and detailed feedback! We're glad to hear you enjoyed the balance of platforming, puzzles, and claw machine gameplay. It’s great to know the progression and physics felt fair and engaging — we’ve worked hard to ensure each mechanic contributes to a rewarding experience. We truly appreciate your support and look forward to hearing your thoughts once you complete the full journey!  🌼

reviews.review_description: Received the game free for preview. The actual game is cute and fun for teenagers and those who want to stay young. You guide a bear through the levels in search of the disappearing animals and rescue them, while solving puzzles. The game is well done, cute art style and works well. What I personally did not like was that after each level, you come to a claw machine and have to fish out multiple stuffed animals. This is just as annoying as in the arcade, if not even more because I personally did not understand why it fits in the story or what it adds to the game. Doing the claw machine once or as an optional thing would be fine, but you have to endure this part to progress.

developer_response.body: Hi QuinrodD, Thank you for the honest feedback! We're glad you enjoyed the puzzle gameplay and art style. We understand the claw machine isn’t for everyone — it's meant to be part of the story and reward system, but we appreciate your thoughts and will keep them in mind as we improve the experience.

reviews.review_title: A great start - Moss meets cranes
reviews.review_description:
Note: Product received for free for testing.

If you like crane games and you like Moss-style 3D platformers with interaction with the level, this is shaping up to be a good game.  I'm being generous in my review rating because this is releasing as Early Access and these issues can be fixed later (and I will update my review to reflect that as I can).

The platforming ramps up appropriately and it's certainly interesting and fun despite the control issues.  The crane game physics are great and feel more realistic (but thankfully not perfectly realistic) than most crane games I play in video games.  The characters are also cute.

Okay, now the negatives:  Controls are wonky with detection seeming to be an issue?  I stop repeatedly as if my controller's stick is dying or I'm having tracking issues but I know for a fact that it works fine.  (They've improved the crane stick detection so I'm hoping they improve that too.)  It's very obvious a lot of content is missing with goals to find legendary plushes that never show up and get to levels that don't exist yet and the overall feeling after beating it feels like I played the introduction of a game.  (I would expect to be about 1/3 through the story at most.  It almost feels like a tech demo.)  The crane upgrades don't really make an insane difference and I hope the devs look into Japanese crane games for inspiration for how they could get creative with future levels.  It also seems to be using AI for art/VA but I'm not 100% sure.  There are also audio issues of things like the characters being rescued being like 2x the volume of the game.  There are also broken English phrases they say so hopefully localization is worked on.  

Lots of blemishes but I do think the game has a lot of potential.  I'd personally recommend getting it if the premise intrigues you but possibly wait until after Early Access if you're on the fence.  It's very accessible to new players and it has a bit of something for experienced players.

developer_response.body:
Hi AroraMew,

Thank you very much for your detailed and constructive feedback — we truly appreciate the time you took to share your experience. We're glad to hear that the core gameplay and charm of the characters stood out, and we're especially grateful for your support during Early Access.

We're actively working on improving controls, addressing localization and audio balance, and expanding the overall content to deliver on the full vision for the game. Your comments about claw machine variety and long-term progression are especially helpful as we continue development.

Thanks again for testing and reviewing — your insights are helping us shape a better experience for everyone!

reviews.review_description: This is a good combination of claw machine game and 3rd person platformer, my favorite is the claw machine part and it's quite challenging at times which is quite nice for anyone who likes a bit of a challenge but then there is the 3rd person platform part which works well in general, but just wish the animals you were saving didn't immediately scream for help and annoy me when rescuing them, other than that I personally don't like 3rd person games in VR but I'm sure if you like those kind of VR games you will like this as well, I can definitely say I enjoyed the claw machine gameplay and feels like it's quite well done. Other than the facts mentioned, I think it is a quite enjoyable game.

Bearly escape is a game that will delight adults and children alike. The game has two relatively simple gameplay phases: the first consists of a claw game like you'd find at a funfair, where you have to catch various stuffed animals to earn bonuses, while the second phase is a 3d platformer in which you control a teddy bear who has to save his stuffed animal friends by facing enemies and traps along the way.
Both phases are perfectly executed, with the controls responding to the touch of a finger.
And what can I say about the design of the teddy bears? they're so cute! (The rendering of the fur is excellent!).
Congrats to the developers, and I hope to see more content in the future.

It's probably the cartoony graphics or maybe the claw machine, but it just brings out your inner child. It's a fun, relaxed, and brain exercising game. This game will make you think in ways you wouldn't expect. I bear-ly (hahaha...get it?) finished the later levels in time. I highly recommend this game to anyone looking for a board game feeling VR game. It has a pretty good storyline, too! The claw machine was so fun to use. It wasn't too hard to grab the stuffed animals inside. Have you ever not been able to get the one thing you want in a claw machine? Well, then play the claw a few times and get a hammer. Smash the glass on the claw and grab that toy you really wanted.

Note: The game was provided to me for free for early testing.

developer_response.body:
Thank you — this absolutely made our day! 😄
We love that it brought out your inner child (and that bear-ly pun was perfect 🐻👌).
So glad you enjoyed the claw machine... and yes, sometimes a hammer is the answer. 🛠️🎁
More fun on the way!

reviews.review_description:
Disclosure:  I was provided a copy of this game for free in order to play test and review.  My opinions are 100% my own. 

Bearly Escape is a fairly simple VR platformer game suitable for all ages.  You’ll control a bear to help him save his friends from the bad guy. There are lots of various platform puzzles along the way as well as a claw machine that helps your character advance through the story.  The game can get repetitive but that’s not necessarily a bad thing because it’s clearly meant for a younger player base.

Bearly Escape’s graphics are bright cheerful but lack some texture and detail that I was hoping to see.

If you’re looking for a game for a younger VR enthusiast, you came to the right place.

Received the game for previewing purposes, honestly very excited to see where this goes, compared to some other vr games I played this one is definitely something I will come back to eventually, I just hope that the ai elements are only temporary but aside from that I see nothing but smooth sailing for this game

reviews.review_title: Fun but controls a bit clunky.
reviews.review_description: Fun little game, my favorite part was the music really well done audio for this title. The gameplay was also a good mix/progression when it came to the platforming elements and enemies. The claw grabber sections are as fun/infuriating as any claw machinr is IRL. My only reel issue with the game is the lag in the camera when playing in smooth turning and I'm sure the devs will smooth that out over time. Regardless a good little time sink with a pleasant soundtrack to play along too.

developer_response.body:
Thanks a lot!
Glad you enjoyed the music and gameplay — we’re working on that camera issue.
More coming soon!

reviews.review_title: A charming game that needs more stuffing
reviews.review_description:
Bearly Escape is a charming little game where you are controlling a teddy bear who is trying to help other stuffed animals escape an evil doctor. The idea is cute, and the concept has potential, but the game is feeling a bit too light and is lacking some focus in my opinion.

The game switches between platforming levels, VR escape room style puzzles, and a claw machine section. To me, this feels a little awkwardly mixed together, and there isn't really a coherent whole. Each of these aspects is also a little too easy and barebones to really stand out to me.

The best of these three sections are the puzzles in my opinion, which are also quite easy and there's unfortunately only a handful of them in the game.

The platforming sections are also ok, but the levels are bite-sized and feel quite easy and a little bit boring in the end.

The worst part in my opinion is the claw-machine section. You are asked to grab animals from a claw machine, which first of all just feels out of place in the game world, and after one or two times this quickly becomes very repetitive and starts to feel like a chore. The claw machine was also a little glitchy for me, where it was constantly dropping the animals I grabbed, which just became frustrating.

In the end, I think the game is cute and looks good, but it is a bit too lackluster for me to be able to recommend it, but maybe it would cater better to a younger audience. There's definitely some fun to be had with this, but I think it needs some polish and depth.

developer_response.body:
Thanks a lot for the detailed thoughts — we truly appreciate it.
We hear you on the depth and cohesion, and we're actively working to improve both.
More polish and content are coming soon!

reviews.review_description:
Bearly Escape is a VR adventure that wants to be loved and often earns the affection it seeks. The warm tone, storybook visuals, and creative claw machine rescues make a strong first impression. Even when pacing slips or the plot loses focus, the game’s heart often comes through. It is easy to root for it, even in its current state.

The claw mechanic stands out as the highlight. Grabbing animals feels satisfying, and the upgrades and changes to each claw scenario keep this part of the game feeling fresh and consistently fun. Losing an animal mid-flight is as frustrating and motivating as it is in real life. The bear platforming is simple but generally pleasant. The art style is gentle and appealing, while the music and sound fit the atmosphere well.

However, the three main gameplay modes do not feel naturally connected. Each mode works on its own, but the transitions between first person puzzles, bear platforming, and claw machine segments feel mixed. As a result, the game sometimes feels like a collection of good ideas that never fully intersect.

The story begins with promise but quickly loses focus. For example, the main antagonist appears early as a generically evil menace to the woodland creatures, but their motivation is unclear, and so is the general narrative. This lack of focus makes it hard to follow and harder to care about the unfolding events.

Movement outside of bear control often feels slow and imprecise. I found myself stopping and adjusting position just to interact with the crane or navigate certain areas. These small frustrations interrupt the flow. Puzzle and platforming elements also appear too frequently and begin to feel repetitive, which dulls the sense of discovery. More variety in these segments would help sustain engagement.

Bearly Escape is a charming and creative experience. It is a title that shows promise and delivers moments of genuine fun, even if the whole is not yet greater than the sum of its parts. 
Note: I received the game as part of a play test.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for this thoughtful and well-articulated feedback.
We’re really glad the charm and creativity came through — and we truly appreciate your insights on pacing, story clarity, and gameplay cohesion. They give us a lot to reflect on as we continue improving the game. 🐻💛

reviews.review_description: This game is visually stunning. It’s a very fun platform / puzzle game with a claw machine! I keep going back in for the scenery and stay for the fun. The game is 3rd bear (person) perspective and is challenging enough to make me go back and try things again sometimes. Save the animals!

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much!
We’re thrilled you’re enjoying the mix of scenery, challenge, and… claw machines! 🐾
More adventures are on the way! 🎉

reviews.review_title: A Platforming Collecter for all ages
reviews.review_description:
Bearly Escape is a really fun and interesting puzzle platformer. I really liked the different sections of the game that kept things interesting. 

An evil scientists wants to rid the world of cute and cuddly animals and guiding your teddy bear across beautifully crafted wooded forests platforming sections while you watch from above is really cool (gives it a Lucky's Tale vibe). Along with other puzzles to open doors and a Claw Machine game that helps you gain collectables and powerup to use in the world.

For an early access game, it didn't have any major issues in my gameplay, and the only real negative was that the textures did seem to have a bit of a pop-in and low resolution to them, more than I would have thought.

Bearly Escape is a very creative and fun game to play for those who are of a younger age that want to dip their toes into some truely 3D platforming. I highly suggest giving it a try!

developer_response.body:
We’re really happy you enjoyed the mix of platforming, puzzles, and claw machine fun. 
Appreciate the note on textures — we’re continuing to optimize visuals during Early Access. More improvements to come! 🐻🌲

reviews.review_description:
This game gives me a feeling of Moss crossed with Astro Bot and a little bit of Max Mustard, all with an overarching storyline where you are the main character and not the bear you control.

In-between the adventure levels there is a puzzle to solve to get back into the main area, which start easy, and gradually become harder. Think The Witness style puzzles, and then to top it off, you have to then rescue all the friends you find along the way from an ever evolving claw machine!

There is a lot to do here and great for both beginners and VR veterans.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much — what an awesome comparison!
We’re honored to be mentioned alongside Moss, Astro Bot, and Max Mustard. 😊
So glad the mix of perspectives, puzzles, and claw machine rescues resonated with you — more updates are coming!

reviews.review_title: A casual super cute fun platformer
reviews.review_description:
Played on a Quest 3

Bearly Escape is a casual game that is split into two parts (platform and claw prize machine), which involves rescuing animals (that look like cuddly toys) who have been caged up by an evil scientist.

The game is fairly short (took me 4-5 hours) it probably would have been shorter if not for the claw machine sections.

This game seems aimed at a younger audience who may get frustrated with the claw machine and struggle with the later levels. 

All in all a quite a fun game with good graphics but I did find the claw machine quite frustrating at times and occasionally unpredictable. I came across no serious bugs or performance issues. Also there is a little bit of replay value in the form of time trials and collecting all the animals.

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for playing and sharing your thoughts! 🐾
We’re glad you enjoyed the adventure overall — and totally hear you on the claw machine feedback. We’re continuing to fine-tune it to make sure it’s fun (not frustrating!) for everyone. Hope you’ll give it another go when the full update drops — more surprises and polish coming soon!

reviews.review_description:
This is a fun and whimsical platformer with some cool puzzle aspects thrown in to mix up gameplay. It’s still early in development, so there’s room to grow (more content, bug fixes etc.)

I’m personally not a fan of the claw machine-type game, which is a large part of the gameplay, but there is also a decent mix of platforming and light puzzling. 

Overall it’s probably not my genre, but I think it’s well made for people to whom it would appeal, the characters are very fun, and it has a unique story theme to it.

developer_response.body:
We really appreciate the honest feedback — and we’re glad you still found some fun in it, even if it’s not your usual genre. 😊
More content and polish are on the way!

reviews.review_title: Great platformer and unique claw machine
reviews.review_description: This game has a fairly interesting and easy to follow story aswell as very intriguing claw machine physics and a great platformer levels. I wouldn't expect to like the claw machine part as much as I did but it feels so real and fair and you can unlock lives to help you in the platformer levels. The platformer levels are decent and have a decent amount of unique components to them though they are pretty short and most of the playtime in my experience came from the claw machines. The game makes great use of the face buttons and joysticks as a normal platformer would but you also have interesting usage of the motion controls to move platforms and portals. I'd say this is something new and if you like platformers this is a pretty good option and I'd suggest it to anyone who is in search of a great VR platformer with new content.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the great review! Glad you enjoyed both the platforming and claw machine—more content is coming soon! 🐻🎮

reviews.review_description:
Bearly Escape is a fun little VR platformer where you help a plush bear rescue other stuffed animals. You move platforms around to help the bear get through levels. There’s also arcade claw machine mini game where you grab more plushies to get tools and energy, which adds a nice break between levels. Coins recharge over time, so you’re not stuck grinding too much!

The game’s still in Early Access, but it’s got a lot of charm. The visuals are super cute, the puzzles add a bit of variety, and the challenge picks up nicely as you go. Overall, it’s one of those games that’s easy to get into and hard to put down. Definitely worth checking out if you enjoy creative platformers in VR.

developer_response.body:
Thank you!
We’re so happy to hear you enjoyed the puzzles, plushies, and platforming! 😊
More polish and content on the way — and we're glad it’s already hard to put down. 🐻🎮

reviews.review_description:
In this game, it's quite apparent that this game is
built to cater for new vr players


However, even if this was the case, I still
managed to enjoy it. It as pretty much every sort
of way you could play VR inside of the game to
properly introduce a user to VR without stacking
any uneccesary pressure onto the user.


However, this game only does this to an extent
since more minigames get more attention than
others.


Also, if you hate Al, please think twice about
playing this game XD


Overall, three stars: ⭐️⭐️⭐️

developer_response.body:
We’re really glad it felt welcoming for new VR players — that was a big goal for us. 
And haha, fair warning on the AI… they do get a bit clingy sometimes.

reviews.review_description: Bearly Escape is a charming and unexpected treat, especially given that it’s still in early access. This single player puzzle platformer blends first and third person gameplay in a way that feels both refreshing and enjoyable. The "bear buddy" levels could benefit from being a bit longer, and the difficulty curve could be tuned to increase a bit more steadily just enough to keep players challenged without becoming frustrating. Overall, it’s a fun and engaging experience.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much! We're glad you enjoyed the mix of perspectives — and totally hear you on level length and difficulty tuning. We're working on it and hope the full release brings an even better experience!

reviews.review_title: A Craning Shame: A Bear-Bones Platformer
reviews.review_description:
Early Release Game Review: Bearly Escape

Full disclosure: I was provided a copy of this game for free to playtest and review. 

In Bearly Escape, players are helping woodland creatures escape the clutches of the maniacal Dr. Kidd. They accomplish this through a combination of platforming puzzle sections and crane machine sections that have you maneuvering a claw to grab stuffed-animal versions of these woodland creatures. 

Pros: 

- The graphics are decent. 
- Gameplay is easy for newer gamers. 

Cons:

- The game has minor graphical glitches (they are being addressed). 
- The game is rather short. 
- The puzzles and platforming are not challenging for seasoned gamers. 
- The non-platform movement options could be expanded to include faster turn speed and improved mobility. 
- The text has grammatical typos (localization needed). 
- Why does the game require internet connection (this game shouldn’t need it). 

Subjective: 

- I found the story to be far too simple for my tastes and I wasn’t emotionally invested in any of the characters. 
- The lead bear’s voice can be grating, screaming, “please help!” each time you rescue an animal. 
- The crane machine segments feel like they artificially extend gameplay length. 

Summary:

I was looking forward to trying this game, but sadly the current product (which is still early access) falls short of standing out from the VR platforming crowd. The platforming sections are simple and not challenging (beyond moving some objects manually, VR-specific interaction feels limited), and the crane sections (while novel at first) feel like they artificially extend the game length with an upgrade system that demands patience without providing much of an incentive beyond more levels of the same, and then it ends rather abruptly. 

One hopes that the developers continue to improve and expand on the story and gameplay they presented here, the biggest need here are levels that make better use of VR-specific controls and challenges while enhancing the story beyond collecting/rescuing animals. 

Given the lead platforming character is a teddy bear, this game could benefit from some dreamlike surrealism: counting sheep, build-a-bear customizations, and fantastical (non-woodland) locations. Really lean into the concept in some fun ways (have animals with stitches or recover stuffing for some of them, having sewing sections, innovate). 

Since I cannot give 2.5 stars, I will round the score to 3 stars and hope that the gameplay and story improve with time.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the detailed review and thoughtful suggestions — we truly appreciate it.

Since the version you tested, the game has gone through multiple updates with improvements in both content and quality. By the time of our full launch, Bearly Escape will look and feel significantly upgraded, including the addition of a final boss level to bring the story to a stronger close.

We’d love for you to come back and give it another try — we think you’ll be pleasantly surprised!

reviews.review_description: Bearly escape is a fun game for the whole family it kind of reminds me of a fairy tale that you grew up with but in  VR form where you help the bear out with puzzles.with  the help of your trusty sidekick your dog robin by your side . I like that you rescue animals that are kidnapped/ trapped in the claw machine by the evil Dr Kidd you use a drone claw to rescue them which is something new that I really like .I like that it has a story with the game which is a plus in my book .the puzzles are just right and the keep your attention and the background music is just right all in all this is a fun game with great graphics and a story to go along with it.

developer_response.body:
Dear skyvuc,
Thank you so much for the kind words! We're thrilled to hear the story, puzzles, and overall experience resonated with you and your family. Creating a whimsical, story-driven adventure in VR has been a core goal for us, and your support means a lot!

reviews.review_description: I didn't know what to expect, and was pleasantly surprised.  I found that the gameplay controls were very realistic, and that the puzzles were more challenging that I would have thought.  I think that the claw game concept is really cool, but at times it was a little too realistic, making it hard to complete.  Regardless, the game has a great look and feel and I'd definitely recommend for kids of all ages.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the great feedback! 😊 We're so glad the experience surprised you in a good way. We'll keep tuning the claw mechanics to balance realism and fun—hope you come back for more surprises in the full release! 🐻🎮

reviews.review_description:
Bearly Escape is a charming VR platformer where you play as a teddy bear rescuing animals from claw machines. The concept is fun and the mix of light puzzles platforming and arcade style mechanics keeps things interesting at first.

Visually  it’s colorful and playful but I did notice some stuttering that made the experience a bit uncomfortable. I actually got a headache after a short session which is unusual for me in VR.

Gameplay is entertaining but lacks real depth. The puzzles are pretty easy and the story feels secondary. It’s a good game for quick, casual sessions but probably not something I’d keep coming back to for long.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the thoughtful feedback! We're glad you enjoyed the concept and playful visuals — and we're actively working on performance improvements to make the experience smoother. Appreciate you giving it a try!

reviews.review_description:
I have played this game for quite a few hours and I really enjoyed it. It is for ages young to elderly and you can get immersed in the game quite easily. The character design is perfect for this game.

It has a story line of helping to save other animals with puzzles to figure out. Then it has a claw machine that will test your physics and also has puzzles. There are constant surprises as you move through the game and keeps you coming back for more.

This game is in early access, so you might see some glitches or bugs. I experienced some trouble with gripping the lever to move the claw machine sometimes but it wasn't a deal breaker for me. As the developers continue to update the game I am positive this will be one of the best games I will have to play.

developer_response.body: Hi KRGoldy, many thanks  for the thoughtful and encouraging feedback! We're thrilled to hear you enjoyed the game and felt immersed in the experience. The bear and animal rescue story means a lot to us, and we’re working hard to keep improving — especially with small issues like the claw lever. Your support truly motivates our team!

reviews.review_description: Early Access playformer game that is fun. VR adventure puzzler where you rescue the animals. And using the claw machine is fun and can be more challenging the further you get. there's leader boards for animal collections and level completion time scores as well. I enjoyed my time in this game and the only thing I seemed like could be better was smooth turning while walking features with both joysticks. move with one and turn with the other is what I prefer.

developer_response.body: Wow, your kind words made our day! It means the world to us that you enjoyed the animal - rescuing adventure and the claw machine fun. We're constantly aiming to level up the gaming experience, and your support is what keeps us going strong. Here's to many more exciting updates and adventures together!

reviews.review_description: I had a great time with the platforming sections, but they weren’t exactly challenging. It’s a fun game if you’re hosting a bunch of kids and need something to keep them entertained. But overall, it’s a decent game and not great.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for giving it a try!
We’re glad the platforming was fun, even if not too challenging — definitely aiming to make it accessible for all ages. 🐻
Appreciate the honest feedback!

reviews.review_description: Bearly Escape is a pretty fun game! The game mashed together a few elements: a claw machine and a 2.5D platformer. However, I do get the feeling of almost a mobile game because of limited lives, it is still fun in the platformer parts.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for playing!
We’re so happy you enjoyed the platformer parts — and we totally get the mobile vibe too! 😊
We’re still evolving the balance, so your feedback helps a lot. 🐻💡

reviews.review_description:
The claw machine physics are no joke. Feels like you need an engineering degree to get that perfect drop. I respect it.
This is already so interesting in EA, its official version must be even more interesting, I am looking forward to it

developer_response.body: Hi ReturnLegend, thank you so much for the kind words and support! We're thrilled to hear you're enjoying the claw machine mechanics — they’re meant to be a little tricky, but all part of the fun! It’s wonderful to know the Early Access version has made a strong impression, and we're working hard to make the full release even more exciting.

reviews.review_description: This game shows how to do 3rd-person platformers right and then adds a little puzzling and a claw machine on top.  It feels very polished and well thought out with a gradual build-up in difficulty.  People who gravitate to action-packed FPS games might not find it to their taste, but if you like more laid-back games you should give it a try.  I think kids would really like it for the cuteness but it can be a relaxing game for adults too.  You are on a timer for the claw machine and the platforming but it isn't a stressful timer, just a good reminder to keep moving.  If you don't get a good grip on your targeted animal with the claw machine you can drop it, just like a real-life claw machine.  I never had a problem of having too few coins to feed the machine so the accidental drops didn't bother me.  For the platforming part there were plenty of restore points so failures don't force you to re-do the whole course.

developer_response.body: Hi, Mepper! We really appreciate your detailed feedback! Balancing charm, pacing, and challenge was key to our design. We’re thrilled it worked for you.

reviews.review_title: A great start to an Early Access title
reviews.review_description:
The claw machine physics are no joke. Feels like you need an engineering degree to get that perfect drop. I respect it.
It’s like two games in one — a chill claw machine sim and a wild forest survival run. Totally didn’t expect to get this hooked.

developer_response.body: Hello CheerfulSalmon5717, we appreciate your support during Early Access and look forward to sharing more soon!

reviews.review_description:
This is an interesting mix of game genres in one. You have the first person story with some door puzzles, there is the 3rd person platformer that has some similarity to Moss, and the claw machine collection game that has various changes on each level. With that, you also have leaderboards for time on levels and animals collected as well as multiple in game achievements.

In it’s current live state as of release, I’d probably say it is a solid 3 stars as there is clearly missing content and you can’t complete all the objectives they have listed. However, having also been part of the testing, I would bump it up another half star, but since we can’t do halves, I’ll round up. The missing content is in development, so there is more to the game. However, the game has a very generic feel to it. 

One thing that makes it feel generic to me is the voice acting and character voices. It has a story that goes along with it, but then along the way just seems to shift to fit into the game. Then, hearing the animals talk that you are saving just sounds like an audio clip playing, not even trying to match the environment that you are in. Like mentioned, the platformer part has some similarity to Moss, but much less detail and feels clunky at times, especially with the platforms that you move by hand. The first person part of moving around also needs some work as it does not feel natural. Like it was made for teleport only and the smooth locomotion was an afterthought that still needs more work. I don’t think I have ever used a rotation method like this one. 

Overall, it’s a fun little game that lasts about 2 hours, but much longer if you want to get into the grind of beating leaderboard times and completing the achievements. It’s still early access and shows in several places. I fully expect that the game will get better over time though.

developer_response.body: Hi, Satconcook! Really appreciate you joining our test and sharing detailed thoughts! We hear you on the voice acting, clunky platforming, and smooth locomotion—these are areas we’re actively reworking. Your feedback helps us a lot, and we hope the full release will feel far less generic and more immersive. Thanks again!

reviews.review_description:
This game definitely exceeded my expectation. ￼ Had alot
Of puzzle solving and saving dem animals. I recommend to all who loves playing as a teddy bear. Look forward to seeing more storylines.

Man, this game’s got me hooked but also stressed—every level feels like a heart-pounding arcade showdown. The claw machines? Perfect difficulty for someone like me who’s obsessed with real-life claw machines (💸🤖) but hates wasting cash or drowning in plushies. If only the claws were a tad gentler… Still, the mix of chaotic platforming and chill collectibles hits just right. Best therapy ever—win a teddy, forget adulting for an hour. Solid 10/10

developer_response.body: Hi, BeanCounterrr! Your review is a ride in itself—and we loved every line! That claw machine stress? Totally intentional 😈 Thanks for letting Bearly Escape be your hour of therapy. Keep grabbing, keep escaping!

reviews.review_description:
This game has a lot of charm; it's a platforming game with claw game elements. In between the areas there is also a bit of puzzling, which adds to the experience. At the moment it's lacking a bit of polish, especially in the instructions front, explaining the gameplay  loop with the crane and how to get coins with different activities, but all the gameplay is quite fun, and works well. The claw is particularly fun, easy to play, but hard to master. Looking forward to seeing how this develops over time.
Disclaimer, I received access to this game for free for playtesting purposes

developer_response.body:
Hi ZombieBear, 
Thank you for the kind words and constructive feedback! We’re glad you’re enjoying the core gameplay and claw mechanics — and we hear you on improving clarity around instructions and the gameplay loop. We're working on it and really appreciate your support during early access!

reviews.review_description:
I played a pre-release beta version of this game and happy to say it was fun enough that I completed it..

I like the split of puzzle platformer for the levels and claw machine mechanic in-between levels. You know my son would probably love this if it was just a claw machine game.. *ha ha*

The character design fits the game style well, and I'd say most kids would enjoy it.

developer_response.body: Hi, ItsVRK! Thanks so much for finishing the beta! We're glad the mix of intense, fast-paced platforming and cute claw machine moments made it a fun ride. Maybe we should consider a "just claw" mode for future updates—your son might be onto something!

reviews.review_description: Growing up, I loved playing the claw game at arcades. This game not only has the claw game, but you get to play with this adorable bear, helping him save his furry friends. This game is claw meets Moss, and im all for it! I love this game, and so do my kids. Don't pass this one up, it's way too cute!

developer_response.body: Hi, Karma77420! So glad the game brought back those arcade claw memories—with a bear-y cuddly twist! Hearing your kids love it too truly made our day. Thank you for playing and sharing the joy!

reviews.review_description:
I received a free copy of this game for play testing.

This rating should be a 4. It's so close! The game has some problems, but most of them aren't too bad. The game feels a bit disjointed. There are several aspects to the gameplay that feel like they were thrown together. Individually the different activities are fun. They are still fun haphazardly joined into a game. It just feels a little "off". There's a weird little story about a mad scientists with a voice actor that really seemed to be having fun with it. Unfortunately these story sections were sometimes triggered right as some other event in the game took over the screen. You could still hear the story, but again it was a little off.

There are basically three styles of gameplay here. You have the claw machine. The physics on that is actually pretty impressive. It can be frustrating, but you get plenty of coins, so it's better than the real thing. Then there are an assortment of door unlock puzzles. These are all pretty light, but they seemed to stop being part of the game pretty early. I wish there were more of them. Finally, there are platforming levels. This was my favorite part and that bear is so cute! It's not as polish as something like Moss, but I really enjoyed it until the level timer started getting shorter and was running out before I could finish. I don't know why they put the timer in there. It's not fun, but it doesn't ruin the game either.

So if it was just all that, I would give it a 4. It's got some flaws, but when it really comes down to it, I had a lot of fun. Unfortunately sometimes you have to move yourself through the VR world. It's not even an important part of the gameplay, but the smooth movement is broken. You have to turn it on in the opportune because it defaults to teleport and I should have left it there. There is no strafe movement. There's just turn and move forward and back on the same stick. It makes it a chore to move around and position yourself. This is only when you move yourself; the platforming movement is fine, but the way you move yourself really bugs me.

developer_response.body:
Hi Ttpancoast,
Thank you so much for your honest and thoughtful feedback — it really means a lot to us. We’re happy you had fun, and we hear you clearly on the movement issues, game flow, and other areas that felt a bit off. We're actively working to improve these, and your input truly helps us make the game better.

reviews.review_description:
I don't usually play puzzle VR games but this one was a lot of fun. I liked the back and forth from claw machine to guiding the bear through the maps. Game constantly added new features as I progressed, and when I was thinking about jumping off it opened up even more features that kept me playing longer.
Definitely a game l'd put a beginner VR player in as it shows a whole different aspect of VR than most games show. Very fun and worth the price!

developer_response.body: Hi, DenimDanger0501! Really appreciate your kind words! Our goal was to create a charming, accessible experience with growing complexity for players of all backgrounds. It's encouraging to hear it resonated, and we're always listening as we continue to refine the game. Thanks for sticking with the bear until the end!

reviews.review_description:
This is an interesting genre mix. The main game is the claw machine, and not the platforming. Yes there are platforming levels, but I wouldn't mind if they were larger. They are cute, contain puzzles and you can unlock items.
The claw machine gives you extra points when you collect the stuffed (really cute) animals of a certain kind in a row. Depth perception can be a little tricky imo, but it's manageable.
Great game for kids and definitely cheaper than a real claw machine.

developer_response.body: Hi, Tryyton! Thank you for the great review and the helpful notes! We're glad the gameplay appeals to kids (and wallets!). Your point about the platforming level size and depth perception is well taken—we're exploring ways to expand levels and improve spatial clarity, especially in VR. Your feedback helps us make Bearly Escape even more enjoyable across all age groups.

reviews.review_description: Alright, this game is super cute. *Bearly Escape VR* drops you into a fun messy world where you're trying to save forest animals. A huge mean claw machine has turned them into stuffed toys. The puzzles are smart, the VR platforming works well, and the whole thing is just silly and sweet. If you're into cuddly critters playful jokes, and a touch of arcade memories, you should give this one a look.

developer_response.body: Hi, csonis! We’re so happy you enjoyed the chaos and cuteness combo—that’s exactly the spirit we hoped to capture! Thank you for highlighting what worked well for you. We're continuing to polish and expand on that whimsical world, and your kind words keep our team motivated to deliver even more joy (and cuddles) in future updates.

reviews.review_title: going walking with the dog in the wood..
reviews.review_description:
all animals are gone if you young and feel the help out all the animals as a bear as third person action game than this is your new mission also you have to be a certified crane operator to take out all the animals without hurting them.
and there is a great board of scores trophy's and bonus you can fill out 
so go in and dont fall down ;-((

developer_response.body: Hi, McCoreBe! Thanks for your creative and heartfelt comment! You really captured the playful spirit of the game—helping animals and mastering the claw machine as a determined little bear. We love how you dove into the world with imagination. Keep exploring (and try not to fall)! 😊

reviews.review_description: If you enjoy claw machines this game will be right up your alley. But, that’s only part of the gameplay. After you catch animals in the claw machines you also have the opportunity to go rescue animals in a “moss” like environment. You control a bear in platforming/puzzle gameplay. Once you rescue the animals you go back to the claw machine. As the game progresses you face other obstacles and challenges with the claw machine.These changes make the game more challenging and keeps you coming back.  The only issues I’ve faced were animals in the claw machine being slightly out of reach. With perseverance I ended up winning. It’s a really fun game with different gaming mechanics to keep you playing.

developer_response.body:
Hi Kubo92,
We're glad you enjoyed the blend of claw machine and Moss-style gameplay — and kudos for sticking with those tricky catches! We’ll keep working to fine-tune the experience and add more variety as the game evolves. Thanks!

reviews.review_description:
Bearly Escape delivers an adorable puzzle-platforming adventure with engaging character interactions and fun animal collection mechanics. The animations bring the bear protagonist to life, making the first interactions truly memorable. The early puzzles are clever but don’t carry through to the later rescue missions, which could benefit from more varied mechanics beyond the hammer and boomerang tools.

While the experience is delightful, the limited levels and lack of replayability mean I’ve completed everything without much incentive to return (aside from leaderboard chasing, which isn’t my thing). A few more levels and integrated puzzle challenges would make this a perfect VR escape. That said, if you’re looking for a heartwarming adventure with a lovable bear companion, Bearly Escape is a great choice!

developer_response.body: Hi, motion_Megan! Thank you for the thoughtful and detailed review—it really means a lot to our team. We’re happy to hear the characters and early puzzles left a strong impression! We also take your suggestions seriously: adding more fun content in the next few levels are exactly the kinds of updates we’re planning. Stay tuned—we hope to surprise you with more meaningful content soon.

reviews.review_description: I received a copy of this game for free to playtest it. It's a mash-up of a claw machine and a 3D platformer with some little puzzles sprinkled along the way. Very family-friendly, an excellent one to let your kids run wild in. Graphics look good, physics are smooth, and the platforming/puzzles are low-medium difficulty.

developer_response.body: Hi,preso.millswords! Thanks so much for playtesting and sharing your thoughts! We're glad you enjoyed the charm and accessibility—perfect for younger players. That said, the early levels are just a warm-up—the later ones offer more surprises and even challenge seasoned players a bit more. We truly appreciate your feedback and hope the full journey brings even more joy and wonder.

reviews.review_description: I was looking for a game that I could play with the kids that wasn't zombies or MCU/DCU yet still entertaining for me and kids ranging from 8 to 12. I like the mixture of games/puzzles and playing as the bear. The kids dig it and I get to amaze them with my claw machine skills. I did experience some issues with the controllers where it seemed a little off, but it seemed fixed the next time I played it. All around a good time for family fun!

developer_response.body: Hi, Dadjokesrule! Thanks so much for playing with your family! We're so glad Bearly Escape gave your family a fun and goofy time together. Playing claw machine battles with your kids sounds perfect! We appreciate you flagging the controller issue—we’re actively improving things like that. Thanks again for playing and for choosing our game for your family night!

reviews.review_description: The animals are so cute in the claw machine...I just want to rescue them all! Looking forward to see how this shapes up during early access. The platforming levels are a bit short sometimes, but I love the mix up between puzzles, crane game and platforming.

developer_response.body: Hey PushDustIn! Thanks a ton for sharing your thoughts and the kind words about our game. We're over the moon that you dig the blend of puzzles, crane game mechanics, and platforming. We totally get what you mean about the platforming levels feeling a bit short, and we're already hard at work brainstorming ways to beef them up and make the experience even more epic during early access. Your excitement is fueling our passion, and we can't wait to show you all the cool stuff we have in store. Stay tuned!

reviews.review_description:
Game received for free to test it.

I didn't expected much from it, as it felt obvious for me that the game target was the kids. I was wrong. The game is funny and pretty cool. The gameplay is really nice and I loved the puzzles levels. Yes the game target mostly young peoples, but it still please this old folk. I recommend this game if you love puzzles.

developer_response.body:
Hi omegaprimal77，
Thank you so much! We're thrilled it surprised you in a good way — while it’s made for younger players, we love hearing that puzzle fans of all ages can enjoy it too!

reviews.review_description:
It's nice to have some deviation from traditional
platformers, which is where the crane games
make a nice variation. Unfortunately I don't feel
the crane game has a big impact on the gameplay, though.
It generally feels a little rough and unpolished here and there but overall it's a fun little game.

developer_response.body: Hi, Yojeyu! Appreciate your thoughtful review! We've heard similar thoughts about the crane game’s limited impact, and we’re working to make it more than just a bonus—by integrating it into level mechanics and reward systems. The rough edges you noticed are also a top priority as we improve polish. Thanks again for playing and giving us a clear direction to work on!

reviews.review_description: a straightforward little game like moss combined with a claw machine arcade game. every level starts with a claw game that has different elements to make it harder on higher levels. then you play a 3d person minigame where you control a bear to save the other animals on the levels. it's pretty fun and simple with a nice little story to explain what's happening.

developer_response.body:
Hi Lavuy67,
Thank you! We're happy you enjoyed the blend of claw machine and platforming — and glad the simple story helped tie it all together!

reviews.review_description:
This is another excellent example of how 3d platforming can excel in vr. Fans of games like Astro Bot and Moss will find plenty to enjoy here. The crisp and adorable levels are really fun to explore and hop around in. It's really satisfying to collect all of your little animal buddies as you go along through the stages. 

The second half of the game is a really addicting claw machine simulator. I used to spend hours trying to win claw machine games as a kid. This game translated that experience to vr extremely well. I've played this game since "early access," but it has always had a real sense of polish and quality to it. I would highly recommend this, especially if you like cute stuff like Nintendo.

developer_response.body: Thank you for such a thoughtful and detailed review! We're honored to be mentioned alongside Astro Bot and Moss — and we're so happy to hear the claw machine brought back childhood memories! Thanks for supporting us since early access!

reviews.review_description: If your looking for something to scratch that Moss itch then look no further.  This platformer will keep you entertained for hours. Need to practice your claw machine skills? Well it's got a very nice/accurate claw machine you get to rule.  This a very cute family friendly game, and now I want all the characters as stuffed animals, not sure if that was the intent ;). Dev's keep up the great work, love what you've created.

developer_response.body: Hi Slim 1337, thank you! So glad you’re enjoying the adventure and bear!

reviews.review_title: Bearly Escape is a pretty original game!
reviews.review_description: Bearly Escape is a pretty original game! I never seen a crane game mix with platforming genre before this game. In this game, you will play a claw machine mini game to get boost to  do the platforming level, which consist of going through a level with different kind of traps. The platforming level is reminding me of Moss, with small puzzle that will keep you on your toes! This is a pretty fun game that I would recommend!

developer_response.body: Hello SirKhalid, thanks for playing! We're glad you enjoyed our unique mix of claw machine and platforming — more surprises to come! 🐾

reviews.review_title: Great game for kids and families!!
reviews.review_description:
The game is pretty good. It's a good platformer title, but the storyline isn't really my style, but I know other people will most likely like it judging by all the positive reviews.

The claw machine is a great mechanic for the game, but it's like one of those real claw machines where the claw doesn't grab by full power, so the stuffed toy could fall down which is really annoying.

They took an interesting way for the cutscenes they used AI to make it, which doesn't look that bad. I actually kind of like it. Also, the kid voice actor really reminds me of the really old Disney Tarzan game, which is really nostalgic.

developer_response.body:
HI ghosp,
Thank you for your feedback! We'll keep improving the claw experience, and we're glad you enjoyed the voice acting and the nostalgia.

reviews.review_description:
Bearly Escape is part Claw Machine part Action
Adventure Puzzle Solver. I very much enjoy
the realistic physics of the claw machine. For
someone who has never been very good at claw
machines, it kept me entertained, and I got quite
good at it!
You find yourself immersed in beautifully
developed atmospheres where the lights and
sounds of the machine help you to celebrate
each animal you save. Collect all the animals
and bring them home!
The puzzles are basic, so this would be a
perfect game for the younger players, and I definitely recommend this title as a family-friendly game.

developer_response.body: Hi LatissaKatrice,  We're thrilled you enjoyed the realistic claw physics and immersive atmosphere — and it’s great to hear it’s fun for all ages!

reviews.review_description:
This game was a total surprise to me i didnt know what to expect when i first started it up following a cute stuffed teddy bear through the woods, hey if nothing else the grandkids will get a kick out of it, two hours later im still playing it, and enjoying myself.  The game has arcade claw machine with pretty accurate physics, once you collect enough stuffed animals from machine each helping you by providing powers for your adventure you are prompted to enter an adventure which is a moss style puzzle platformer where you guide your bear through the platforming area and assist by manipulating objests to help, with your goal to rescue 3 animals that are trapped or blocked and finish before time runs out collecting coins along your path to use in the claw machine for the next level, this is where the game shines each level has different puzzles to solve to complete the mission, there are 12 levels each getting progressively more difficult the claw machine itself also gets more difficult.  
I really enjoyed the chill gameplay loop.  This may look like a kids game on the surface but has strategic puzzle escape room elements with a platforming twist.

developer_response.body: Hello Ellehcim99,  it's awesome to hear the mix of claw machine chaos and puzzle platforming kept you hooked — and that it’s fun for both you and the grandkids! We’re continuing to improve and expand the experience.

reviews.review_description:
This is a great family friendly little platformer.  You play through first person and third person throughout segments of the game.  You are a park ranger searching for the captured forest animals.  You find them turned into stuffed toys by the evil doctor.  
  The levels are basically broken down into the platform part a puzzle and a claw machine mini game.  I was pleasantly surprised how real the machines felt at times.  Though they add slightly more challenges to make it harder the more you progress.  
  The colors and art style are amazing and flow well together while keeping a happy family feel and looks.
  Overall the game is great for early access and I see lots of potential.  I spent way more time than needed playing with the claw games.  However the game is rather short.  I would like to see the number of levels increase.  The leaderboards make a great competitive aspect as well.

developer_response.body: Thank you for playing! We’re thrilled you enjoyed the art style, gameplay variety, and claw machines — we’ll keep working to expand the levels and make it even better!

reviews.review_description: This is a fantastic game for the whole family. The kids were telling me where to take the Bear to play the Claw game, all while running through the forest. It has truly been a great experience for  all of us. The only thought I had was, can the Bear be a little bigger in the forrest?

developer_response.body: Hi, ShadowyChipmunk4069! We’re so happy to hear that the whole family is enjoying the game — that’s exactly what we hoped for when making Bearly Escape! Thank you for the feedback about the bear’s size in the forest — that’s a great note, and we’ll definitely take it into consideration as we continue refining the experience. Sending you and your little claw machine crew a big virtual bear hug!

reviews.review_description:
I really enjoyed playing the puzzles in this game. The claw machine mechanics are also fun. The opening scene was really cute. The puzzles were interesting. The claw mechanics were not that hard to master. 

Loved the unlockables system. Both the skins and upgrades seemed interesting to try and unlock. 

Overall an excellent game.

developer_response.body:
Hi Majed6,
Thank you so much! We're glad you enjoyed the puzzles, claw machine, and unlockables — more surprises and content are on the way!

reviews.review_title: If you go down to the woods today
reviews.review_description:
Bearly Escape has made a decent start for a game in early access, environments look pretty good, the claw machine works well and it offers a good mix of things to do, in earning things for levels via the claw machine and then going through a level while saving animals along the way.
There's a few things I would like changing, the smooth rotation is slow unless you pick the option with both thumbsticks, then turning while using both causes it to rotate at warp speed, I'd really just love the standard forward and back on the left thumbstick and turning on the right.
I play seated, but the seated option feels way too low, it kind of feels like seated and standing are reversed, so maybe rise and lower using A/B X/Y would be good.
Also the game currently just kind of ends, it's definitely in need of a boss fight, which it's alluding to during the story so hopefully that gets added to really give the game a proper ending.

developer_response.body:
Hi AllUAreIsHistory,
Thank you for the thoughtful feedback! We're glad you enjoyed the environments, gameplay loop, and claw mechanics. Your notes on improvements , especially the need for a proper ending (yes—boss fight!) are all spot on, and the team is already working on them. More to come soon!

reviews.review_description: Bought the game for my daughter because she played it at our neighbors house. I played it first to make sure it was appropriate for her, apparently I got caught playing it a little longer than she was willing to wait, ha! It was a cute game and at a level that both her and I found entertaining. I'm not a gamer and usually try to pass things like this off on my husband but the VR experience was pretty fun. I got caught up in the immersive environment. Again, I don't know much about games but was pleasantly surprised by this game.

developer_response.body: Hi, loca4life! This made our day—thank you! We're so happy the game surprised and delighted you. VR can be magical even for non-gamers, and it's awesome that you found it fun and immersive. Sounds like your daughter may have to share the headset now! Thanks for giving Bearly Escape a chance!

reviews.review_description: Frequent bugs and glitches - would stay away in its current state.

developer_response.body:
We’re really sorry to hear that.
we’re actively fixing bugs and working hard to improve stability with each update.

reviews.review_description: This game has a lot of puzzles that involve critical thinking, but not to the point that your child will spend hours on them and get stuck, they are very child friendly and could definitely help their brains develop well! The overall gameplay is great! Reminds me heavily of Max Mustard/Mario games. There's also a claw machine every level where you can get some more stuffed animals, since claw machines are usually adored by children this seems like a great way to do it as immersive as possible (without paying everytime😆) The only issue I have is that the devs decided to rely on AI for a small portion of the game (cutscenes, Intro, story, etc) I wish they had gotten real artists since that was the only thing keeping it from 5 stars for me. Overall highly recommend if you have children!

developer_response.body:
Hi KindGentlemanThatisntKind,
Thank you so much for the kind words and thoughtful feedback! We're thrilled you found the puzzles and gameplay fun and kid-friendly — that's exactly what we hoped to achieve. We appreciate your note on the cutscenes and will definitely keep that in mind as we continue improving the game. Thanks again for the recommendation!

reviews.review_description: I wasn't sure if I'd enjoy this, but I actually did. The blend of platforming, puzzle-solving, and the claw machine actually does work well and keeps things from getting boring. The claw machine does take some actual precision, which I oddly liked. I played it with my little sister and she enjoyed it—especially the animals in the claw game. It's truly family-friendly but still fun for older players too. Only wish some of the platforming areas were a bit longer, but overall, it's just such a fun game.

developer_response.body:
Hi Draner2010,
Thank you so much! We're really happy to hear you and your sister both enjoyed the experience — that's exactly the kind of family-friendly fun we hoped to create. We’ll definitely keep your feedback in mind as we expand the platforming sections!

reviews.review_title: A nice mix of different genres
reviews.review_description:
At its core it's a platform with interesting levels that vary quite a bit. But between the levels there are story cut scenes where you need to solve simple puzzles and a grabbing hook machine, like the ones often found at theme parks.

I also really like the setting of the game because it's all about animals.

developer_response.body:
Hi Sajeg, 
Thank you! We're happy you enjoyed the mix of gameplay styles and the animal-themed setting — that’s exactly the kind of experience we hoped to create!

reviews.review_description:
As an "early access" game, this shows a lot more polish and consideration than a lot of other games I've picked up on Meta.

Both the graphics and sound are on point which is unfortunately kind of rare because with VR in particular, audio can go a lot ways to creating a spacial and immersive experience.

All that technical stuff aside, this is a really fun puzzle, platformer. The puzzles avoid being repetetive and feel like callbacks to a number of fun puzzle games from the past such as Pipe Dream (or how you'd unlock vaults/doors in the original Bioshock for those are are too young to remember PD) and Chip's Challenge. those are games that are very ascetically different but some of the game-play here reminds me of those.

The platforming is fun but not that difficult which really is fine because I see it as more of a way to link the various puzzles together into a solid narrative. I mention this because if you're looking strictly for a platformer, you might be disappointed, here. That's certainly part of it but I feel like the puzzles are the main attraction.

Again, as a game not yet in full release, I'm really impressed by this one. So much of what's on the Quest is either rhythm, some form of FPS or what feels like someone's experiment in learning how to create something in VR.

This one really feels different and I can't way to see where they take it going forward.

developer_response.body:
Hi ThePromee,
Thank you so much for the thoughtful and insightful review! We're honored by the comparisons to classics like Pipe Dream and Chip's Challenge, and thrilled to hear the polish and variety stood out to you — especially in the context of VR. You're absolutely right that the puzzles are at the heart of the experience, and we’re excited to keep building on that foundation as we move toward full release. Thanks again for playing and for your support!

reviews.review_description: I will admit that this game let me down because of the movement where there is no strafing in the game and you have to turn with the left joystick and move with it also the menu is a disaster it reminds me of a mobile game where it's bombarded with stuff to the side and too many tabs also in the beginning there was only ai voices which did make my head hurt and now they fixed with actual voice acting which is good also they fixed the platforming making it feel smoother and better I wish for the future you could still fix stuff like the menu and the jankiness of the arcade machine

developer_response.body:
Hi Windkif,
Thank you for the honest feedback! We're glad the updated voice acting and smoother platforming improved your experience — and we hear you on the menu and arcade machine polish. More improvements are coming!

reviews.review_description: I am sure it has its own audience, it seems from the reviews people are enjoying the game but it's just not for me. I feel like this game would be great for kids who are into platformers especially with the look of the game I can imagine kids would be more than happy with it as for me I will be sticking to being a claw machine addict when I do decide to hop on!

developer_response.body:
Hi Easier, 
Thank you for giving it a try! Totally understand it's not everyone's style, but we're glad you see the appeal for younger players — and we’ll keep the claw machines ready for your next visit!

reviews.review_description: From the moment I started this game this game had a unique way of a puzzle game with a side scrolling platformer. The use of the claw machine was fun amd engaging. If you are a fan of puzzles and platformer games , then definitely check out this game.

developer_response.body: Hi, Ktech23! Thank you so much! We're really glad you enjoyed the mix of puzzles and platforming. The claw machine twist was one of our favorite parts to bring to life, and it's great to hear it stood out. Hope the full adventure continues to surprise and delight you!

reviews.review_description: I really liked this new game. The dimensions and the graphics were pretty good. The bear was cute and fun to play the game with. I think the volume of the characters in the opening videos could be a little louder. I will play this again!

developer_response.body: Hey Coach.T79! Thanks a ton for taking the time to share your thoughts on the game. We're stoked that you dig the dimensions and graphics, and that our bear buddy made the gameplay a blast for you. We totally hear you on the character volume in the opening videos. It's super helpful feedback, and we'll look into cranking it up in future updates. Your support and enthusiasm mean the world to us, and we can't wait to have you back for more fun! Cheers!

reviews.review_description:
Bearly Escape is a VR platformer a bit like Moss or Max Mustard.  The general idea is you play with a claw machine to collect the stuffed animals to gain power ups, then you enter the level where you control the bear (like you would control Quill from Moss) and use the power ups to save the trapped animals.  There are some light puzzle elements but nothing too difficult.  If you like games such as Moss or Max Mustard there is definitely some fun to be had with this one.

I believe this is an early access title, so there's definitely some room for growth still!

developer_response.body: Hello EpiXan, many thanks for sharing your impressions of Bearly Escape! We're very glad to hear that you found the gameplay and structure enjoyable, especially the combination of claw machine mechanics and light puzzle-platforming. You're absolutely right — the game is currently in Early Access, and we truly appreciate your openness to its continued growth. We're actively working on expanding the content and refining the overall experience, and feedback like yours is incredibly helpful along the way. Thanks again for taking the time to play and share your thoughts!
